title: AgentFlow Nebula Database Schema
description: Describes every CSV table in /database. CSV tables are the single source of truth for structured (tabular) data. Narrative content (identities, instructions, workflows) lives in markdown files and is referenced from these tables via File / Folder / Ref columns. Conversely, instruction/instruction.md tells each persona which tables to read.

conventions.format: CSV with a header row; UTF-8; comma-separated
conventions.column-headers: Title Case With Spaces - every word capitalized, separated by single spaces (e.g. 'Assignee Persona ID'). Header text only.
conventions.id-values: Cell values in '* ID' columns are kebab-case, lowercase, no spaces, unique within their table
conventions.file-refs: Columns whose header ends in 'File', 'Folder', or 'Ref' hold workspace-relative paths to markdown files or folders

config/config.csv.purpose: Application and project-level key/value configuration (e.g., workflow-name) written by setup

config/cicd-config.csv.purpose: Pipeline configuration parameters, infrastructure targets, and deployment strategies.

config/task-status.csv.purpose: Enumeration of allowed task statuses and their order

config/bug-severity.csv.purpose: Enumeration of bug severities (impact) and their target response

config/bug-status.csv.purpose: Enumeration of allowed bug statuses and their order

persona/role.csv.purpose: Roles a persona can hold; each role belongs to one team

persona/persona.csv.purpose: The roster of personas (single source of truth for who exists)

project/project.csv.purpose: Projects under management, scored and ranked by priority. Priority is a 1..N ordinal (1 = highest); rows are kept sorted by it. Project ID is a stable primary key and is NOT reassigned when priority changes. Seven scoring factors are each 0-10; Total Score (%) is their sum expressed as a percentage of the 70-point maximum (e.g. 54/70 = 77%). Weeks to MVP is raw informational weeks (not part of the score; lower is better) and feeds the cashflow model; Speed to MVP is its 0-10 scored form (fewer weeks = higher).

project/phase.csv.purpose: Phases that decompose a project. Rows are ordered by project priority (per project.csv) then by build sequence within each project. Phase No is a global 1..N ordinal reflecting that order; Phase ID is a stable primary key and is NOT reassigned when order changes.

project/task.csv.purpose: Actual tasks within a project — the single dispatch queue (Mensa dispatches only from here; bug.csv is never read in the dispatch loop). Hours is the task's total estimated effort. Task Type classifies the work (Feature / Bug / Chore / Tech Debt); a Bug-type row is the fix-task for a defect recorded in bug.csv and is linked back from that bug's Fix Task ID. Date Started and Date Completed are actuals (ISO dates) stamped by Mensa when a task enters in-progress and done respectively; they pair with task-plan.csv's planned dates for plan-vs-actual velocity.

project/bug.csv.purpose: Defect register — the system of record for bugs, owned by Quasar (QA). A bug is a defect record; the work to fix it is a separate Bug-type row in task.csv, linked via Fix Task ID. Kept out of the dispatch loop so the single-table dispatch (task.csv) stays clean. Severity is impact (S1-S4); Status follows bug-status.csv.

project/dispatch.csv.purpose: Daily ledger of assigned tasks created by the Dispatcher (Mensa) for downstream production personas. Cycle No (1/2/3) records which of the day's three cycle-planning sessions issued the dispatch (Date has no time component, so the cycle cannot otherwise be recovered); it is the dispatch cycle, not necessarily the completion cycle.

project/task-plan.csv.purpose: Day-by-day execution plan for tasks: bin-packs each task's estimated Hours onto working days (Monday-Friday) from the 2026-07-01 start date, capped at 8 hours per day per stream, with overflow carried to the next working day. Auto tasks (task.csv Auto=TRUE, cleared by the production personas' work cycles per workflow/scheduled-task.csv) and manual tasks (Auto=FALSE, done by the founder) are scheduled on independent 8-hour/day budgets, so a single task can appear in multiple rows (one per day it is worked) and a single date can recur across tasks. Rows are ordered by task priority (task.csv order), highest first.

prompt-helper/prompt-helper.csv.purpose: Per-persona prompt library: the initialization prompt, scheduled-task prompt, and export/import prompts for each persona

workflow/scheduled-task.csv.purpose: Recurring jobs run by a persona on a cron schedule. Prompt holds the full task prompt executed at trigger time. Rows are sorted by Cron (hour then minute) for readability. Schedule ID is a descriptive kebab-case key (e.g. mensa-weekly-retrospective) that is unique and stable.

cashflow/cashflow.csv.purpose: Weekly cashflow model for the portfolio rollout over 50 weeks, TRANSPOSED: each row is a line item and each column is a week. The first column 'Line Item' is the row key; remaining columns are W01..W50. Line-item rows: 'Week Ending' (date), 'Active Build', then for each project a 3-row block — '<PID> Accumulative Users' (net active subscribers after churn), '<PID> <Lumino name>' (gross revenue), and '<PID> Product Cost' (that product's Paddle fee + infra increment + prospect-comm increment + one-time domain) — for PD08/PD07/PD09/PD10/PD02/PD03/PD04, then 'Total Gross Revenue', 'Total Active Users', 'Total Paddle Fees', 'Total Product Cost', 'Shared Overhead (Base Infra + Base Prospect)', 'Net Weekly Cashflow', 'Cumulative Cashflow'. A live formula-driven version with full calculation logic is in cashflow/Lumino-cashflow-model.xlsx (Assumptions / Calc / Model / Monthly sheets). Inputs and results are documented in cashflow/assumption.csv.

cashflow/assumption.csv.purpose: Key financial and operational assumptions governing the cashflow model
